key_insight: A detailed finite element model was used to iteratively refine the WST altitude structure so it can support the mirrors while meeting weight, stiffness, stress, and vibration targets.
engineering_relevance: For large telescope structures, early FE-driven sizing of beams, plates, and boundary conditions is essential to reduce mass without compromising alignment accuracy or dynamic stability.
method: Built a detailed FE model of the steel altitude structure with representative operational boundary conditions.
Evaluated structural weight, deformations, stresses, and natural frequencies to identify resonance risks.
Used preliminary beam-section and shell-thickness results to guide mechanical optimization and define design criteria.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the model was used to estimate weight, deformation, stress, and frequency response, and that these results informed a refined structural configuration.
watch_item: Results depend on how well the boundary conditions and preliminary section assumptions represent real operating and assembly conditions.
paper_title: WST, the Wide-field Spectroscopic Telescope: Telescope structure FE analyses
